title: SSRF guard checks hostname string, not resolved IP (DNS rebinding)
description: In media.rs process_remote() and server.rs handle_inbox(), is_private_host() is called with the hostname from the parsed URL. This only blocks private IP literals (e.g. 192.168.x.x) and a handful of hostnames (localhost, *.local, *.internal). An attacker who controls a public DNS name can point it at a private IP and bypass the guard entirely — the guard runs before DNS resolution, so the actual connection target is never checked. Fix: resolve the hostname to an IP before making the connection and verify the resolved IP is not private, or use a crate like trust-dns-resolver with post-resolution IP checks. Alternatively, require an allow-list of trusted outbound domains rather than a deny-list of private IPs.
close_reason: Fixed: shared_inbox SSRF guard, key_owner required, webhook media cap. Remaining (replay, unsigned accept, DNS rebinding, body buffer) accepted as known architectural limitations.
